target.name: Corintis
target.founder: Remco van Erp, Sam Harrison, Elison Matioli

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $11.0B.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $11B

data.narrative:
Corintis is a Series A-stage semiconductor company that has raised $25M to date. The valuation assessment employs a peer-median approach triangulated against a stage-progression method, drawing from a universe of 3 private comparables in the chips and semiconductors sector at similar or adjacent stages (SambaNova Systems at $11.0B Series A, Etched at $5.0B Series B, and Positron AI at $1.0B Series B), alongside public semiconductor benchmarks including NVIDIA, AMD, and Mobileye.

The stage-progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $25M total raised, implying a valuation of $50M. This approach assumes capital raised as a proxy for current valuation and typical downstream financing step-ups. The peer-median method yields a $5.0B implied valuation based on the three-company comparables set, with a range spanning $1.0B to $11.0B. The substantial spread reflects heterogeneity in customer traction and funding stage within the peer set.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $42.5M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The wide dispersion reflects tension between early-stage financing patterns and late-stage peer valuations in a capital-intensive, rapidly consolidating sector. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to competitors, addressable market scope, and path to commercialization.
